title: The Chump

jokes.text: The first time I threw mine away, I thought it was an ant trap. But I figured I'd rather live with ants than with his creepy little disk.
jokes.setup: Toby leaving radon detection kits around the office
jokes.punchline: Michael would rather live with actual pest infestation than deal with Toby's safety measure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael character moment - his irrational hatred of Toby leads to absurd preferences. Well-structured escalation.

jokes.text: The second time... I thought it was one of those, you know, the things you turn over, and it moos, like a cow thing. But upon closer examination, it was another ant trap, so I threw it away.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Michael's confusion about radon kits
jokes.punchline: Michael's bizarre description of a cow toy followed by his continued misidentification
jokes.explanation: Michael's rambling, childlike description is perfectly in character. The escalation builds nicely.

jokes.text: And the third time... I did it out of spite.
jokes.setup: Pattern of Michael throwing away radon kits for increasingly absurd reasons
jokes.punchline: Michael admits to pure petty malice with deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic escalation payoff. The honesty and economy of 'out of spite' is brilliant character writing.

jokes.text: You say radon is silent but deadly, and then you expect me not to make farting noises with my mouth?
jokes.setup: Toby's serious radon safety presentation
jokes.punchline: Michael can't resist the juvenile wordplay opportunity
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael juvenile humor. Predictable pun but perfectly executed for his character.

jokes.text: We're not gonna die of radon. We're gonna die of boredom.
jokes.setup: Toby's serious safety meeting
jokes.punchline: Michael's hyperbolic complaint about meeting tedium
jokes.explanation: Solid office humor. The parallel structure works well and it's quotable workplace comedy.

jokes.text: If I had a gun with two bullets and I was in a room with Hitler, Bin Laden, and Toby, I would shoot Toby twice.
jokes.setup: Hypothetical scenario with history's worst villains
jokes.punchline: Michael prioritizes his petty office hatred over global evil
jokes.explanation: Iconic Office joke. Perfect dark comedy escalation, brilliant character insight, became a cultural touchstone. The absurd prioritization is comedy gold.

jokes.text: I would kill Bin Laden and then Toby.
jokes.setup: Staff trying to make Michael's previous joke more reasonable
jokes.punchline: Michael's 'compromise' still puts Toby second to a terrorist
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains the absurdity while seeming to compromise. Still reveals Michael's priorities.

jokes.text: Curve the bullet like in my favorite James McAvoy film, Wanted.
jokes.setup: Discussion of impossible murder scenarios
jokes.punchline: Jim suggests solving it with movie physics
jokes.explanation: Jim playing into the absurdity with deadpan pop culture reference. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: How about make believe land has anything you want?
jokes.setup: Increasingly ridiculous murder planning discussion
jokes.punchline: Angela's dry dismissal of the fantasy scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela deadpan sarcasm. Her pragmatic dismissal of the nonsense is well-crafted.

jokes.text: You line them all up, you take one bullet, shoot them all through the throat at the same time. Watch this. Phyllis, you're Hitler.
jokes.setup: Dwight taking the murder scenario seriously
jokes.punchline: Casually casting Phyllis as Hitler for demonstration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight moment - his matter-of-fact approach to violence plus casual Hitler casting is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Toby, just do it! ...Ready? One bullet. And...Boom! Puuuhhh! Puuuhh!
jokes.setup: Dwight demonstrating his murder solution with office colleagues
jokes.punchline: Dwight's sound effects and enthusiasm for the violent demonstration
jokes.explanation: Dwight's commitment to the demonstration with sound effects is perfectly cringey and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: The Eagles are doing a theme of ice creams in honor of turning 60. 'Despera-dough. Witchy-womanilla.'
jokes.setup: Office getting ice cream for Michael's heartbreak
jokes.punchline: Terrible Eagles song title puns for ice cream flavors
jokes.explanation: Weak puns but fits Erin's character. The joke relies on bad wordplay without much craft.

jokes.text: After his last breakup, he ate 40,000 calories in three hours.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they're preparing for Michael's heartbreak
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and extreme eating statistic
jokes.explanation: Great specific absurd detail about Michael's dramatic reactions. The precision of '40,000 calories in three hours' is funny.

jokes.text: You probably shouldn't keep a baby up that late, though.
jokes.setup: Pam mentioning being up all night with baby Cece
jokes.punchline: Erin's innocent misunderstanding of how babies work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin naivety. Her innocent misunderstanding of parenting is character-perfect and economical.

jokes.text: No, Toby! No! Out! Get out right now!
jokes.setup: Toby entering during Michael crisis management
jokes.punchline: Entire office immediately ejecting Toby to protect Michael
jokes.explanation: Great ensemble moment showing how well everyone knows Michael's Toby issues

jokes.text: Make a random sound effect, okay? Farting noise, whatever.
jokes.setup: Instructions for dealing with depressed Michael
jokes.punchline: Professional advice to make farting noises
jokes.explanation: Absurd workplace advice. The contrast between professional tone and juvenile solution is mildly funny.

jokes.text: It's reversible, I think.
jokes.setup: Compliment about Michael's tie
jokes.punchline: Michael's uncertain response about his own clothing
jokes.explanation: Small Michael moment showing his uncertainty even about basic things. Understated but in character.

jokes.text: Boi-oi-oi-oing!
jokes.setup: Forced attempt to cheer up Michael with random sound effect
jokes.punchline: Awkward execution of Erin's earlier advice
jokes.explanation: Good callback execution. The awkwardness of following through on the bad advice creates cringe humor.

jokes.text: I will do legal jujitsu on you. Gaah!
jokes.setup: Dwight threatening Angela in their custody mediation
jokes.punchline: Dwight demonstrates 'legal jujitsu' with actual martial arts move
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight mixing literal and metaphorical actions. The physical demonstration of a legal concept is perfectly absurd for his character.

jokes.text: I'd like to see a stool sample.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Dwight's beet juice cleanse in their contract
jokes.punchline: Angela demanding gross medical evidence
jokes.explanation: Angela's prissy character demanding something gross creates good contrast. Economical gross-out humor.

jokes.text: So we're gonna say the most likely scenario is that Michael matured overnight? Well, it happened to Tom Hanks in Big. Exactly. It happened in Big.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether Michael has actually moved past his heartbreak
jokes.punchline: Dwight treating fictional movie plot as valid evidence
jokes.explanation: Great Jim setup with perfect Dwight response. Dwight's literal interpretation of movie logic is character-perfect.

jokes.text: We could order in from Hooters.
jokes.setup: Jim offering to have Michael over for dinner with baby
jokes.punchline: Suggesting inappropriate restaurant for family dinner
jokes.explanation: Mild incongruity humor, Jim suggesting something slightly inappropriate

jokes.text: Billy Joel Rock Band. That... exists? Yes.
jokes.setup: Jim mentioning Billy Joel Rock Band
jokes.punchline: Pam's disbelief that this specific product exists, Jim's confident confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat about oddly specific product existing

jokes.text: She's not invisible, so stop asking silly questions.
jokes.setup: Pam asking if Michael is still seeing Donna
jokes.punchline: Michael's literal interpretation avoiding the actual question
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Michael's evasiveness. The literal interpretation is character-appropriate deflection.

jokes.text: If you ask her husband... or you took a random poll, yeah, it's wrong.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if continuing the affair is wrong
jokes.punchline: Jim's understated confirmation that yes, obviously it's wrong
jokes.explanation: Jim's deadpan moral compass moment. The 'random poll' addition makes it funnier than just stating the obvious.

jokes.text: I ask... everyone in the room, 'are you in a relationship?'
jokes.setup: Meredith defending her sexual ethics
jokes.punchline: Meredith's low bar for moral screening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith character moment. Her minimal moral standards presented as high ethics is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: In Paris, it is rude for a woman to have less than four lovers.
jokes.setup: Ryan defending affairs by comparing to European culture
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up cultural 'fact' about Parisian sexual etiquette
jokes.explanation: Classic Ryan pretentiousness with absurd fake sophistication. The specificity of 'four lovers' and 'rude' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: He's a sports guy. He's scummy. Dogfighting, drugs. They spit.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to justify his affair by demonizing the husband
jokes.punchline: Michael's absurd stereotype culminating in 'they spit'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from serious crimes to petty annoyance. Michael's desperate rationalization is perfectly character-driven.

jokes.text: Ten minutes of silence honoring Michael Jackson.
jokes.setup: Michael declaring a moment of silence to stop criticism
jokes.punchline: Random invocation of Michael Jackson for deflection
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael deflection tactic using random celebrity reference. Shows his desperate avoidance of accountability.

jokes.text: My name is Andy Bernard, and I'm a cuckold. For those of you unfamiliar with William Shakespeare, a cuckold is a man whose woman is cheating on him.
jokes.setup: Andy introducing himself in confessional style
jokes.punchline: Andy's pretentious Shakespeare reference for basic concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy pretentiousness. His need to cite Shakespeare for a simple concept shows his insecurity masked as intelligence.

jokes.text: I would so much rather... play the part on stage.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his experience being cheated on
jokes.punchline: Andy preferring theatrical performance to real emotional pain
jokes.explanation: Insightful Andy character moment. His theater obsession making him prefer fake drama to real life is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: You're Ali Larter. I'm Beyoncé. I am Beyoncé always.
jokes.setup: Discussion of cheating movie dynamics
jokes.punchline: Kelly's absolute certainty about always being Beyoncé
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly vanity moment. Her unwavering belief that she's always the Beyoncé is character-perfect and quotable.

jokes.text: Should punch him in the nose for what he's making me do to her.
jokes.setup: Michael planning to confront the husband
jokes.punchline: Michael blaming the victim for Michael's own choice to cheat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic reversal of responsibility. Michael's complete inversion of culpability is perfect character psychology and darkly funny.

jokes.text: I played batboy in Damn Yankees!
jokes.setup: Andy trying to understand baseball positioning
jokes.punchline: Andy's minor theater role as baseball expertise
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy theater obsession. His tiny role giving him false confidence about sports is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Coaching third base with two arms, two legs, and no heart. Capable of feeling pain.
jokes.setup: Michael observing the husband coaching baseball
jokes.punchline: Michael's cruel assessment focusing on the man's capacity for hurt
jokes.explanation: Dark Michael moment showing his attempt to dehumanize his victim while acknowledging the man's humanity.

jokes.text: I actually designed that chart. Kinda hoping it catches on.
jokes.setup: Gabe mentioning his decision tree chart
jokes.punchline: Pride in bureaucratic flowchart design
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe moment - taking pride in mundane corporate tools

jokes.text: I just hate that part of the job, you know, the power dynamics.
jokes.setup: Gabe having just disciplined Jim and Pam for sleeping
jokes.punchline: Gabe's performative discomfort with authority he clearly enjoys
jokes.explanation: Good Gabe character moment showing his false modesty about power while clearly relishing his authority.

jokes.text: In the occurrence that the child is born an old man, a la Benjamin Button.
jokes.setup: Reading Dwight and Angela's bizarre custody contract
jokes.punchline: Contract provision for impossible movie scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist contract provision. The lawyer's matter-of-fact reading of impossible scenarios is perfectly deadpan.

jokes.text: I don't have $30,000 lying around. I have it buried very deeply, and I don't want to dig past a certain someone to get it.
jokes.setup: Dwight owing money for breaking the contract
jokes.punchline: Dwight implying he buried money with a body
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Dwight humor. The casual implication of murder for financial planning is perfectly crafted character comedy.

jokes.text: You don't pay their players. I know, I know, I know. It--I'm--it- that was a euphemism.
jokes.setup: Michael's accusation about coach paying players being corrected
jokes.punchline: Michael's desperate claim it was intentional wordplay
jokes.explanation: Michael's backtracking when caught being wrong. His claim of sophistication is transparently false but in character.

jokes.text: I bet it's like, 'hey, Donna, why don't you wash the dishes? High-five, Donna. Hey, why don't you bring me my slippers? High-five. I'm taking all the credit.'
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing husband for high-fiving players
jokes.punchline: Projection of credit-stealing behavior onto innocent gesture
jokes.explanation: Michael projecting his own management style onto innocent sports gesture

jokes.text: High-five, Donna. Hey, why don't you bring me my slippers? High-five. I'm taking all the credit.
jokes.setup: Michael imagining the husband taking credit for nothing
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd domestic scenarios ending with 'taking all the credit'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Michael's irrational hatred with specific scenarios

jokes.text: Five times for $30,000? Not a bad stud fee. Better than most horses.
jokes.setup: Contract alternative of sexual favors instead of money
jokes.punchline: Dwight comparing himself favorably to horse breeding rates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic applying farm economics to human sexuality. The proud comparison to horses is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Those lumps are cats, and those cats have names, and those names are Ember, Milky Way, Diane, and Lumpy.
jokes.setup: Dwight complaining about lumpy bed
jokes.punchline: Angela's matter-of-fact explanation of cats in bed with their names
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela cat lady moment. The specific names including 'Lumpy' for a cat that creates lumps is brilliant detail.

jokes.text: Are you guys... Kenny's dads?
jokes.setup: Michael and Andy acting suspiciously at baseball game
jokes.punchline: Coach's logical assumption they're a gay couple
jokes.explanation: Natural misunderstanding based on their strange behavior. The coach's reasonable assumption creates good awkwardness.

jokes.text: We're gay for baseball.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain their enthusiasm after being mistaken for a couple
jokes.punchline: Michael's attempt at clever wordplay that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt to be clever backfiring. The phrase choice makes their situation more awkward, which is perfect cringe.

jokes.text: I am so into the institution of marriage. Two good people finding each other, getting all committed to each other.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to seem like a marriage advocate
jokes.punchline: Michael praising marriage while actively destroying one
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony. Michael's passionate defense of marriage while cheating creates painful, funny hypocrisy.

jokes.text: But as a fan, it just helps me enjoy the game better if I know the coach loves his wife.
jokes.setup: Michael pushing the coach for personal information
jokes.punchline: Michael's absurd reasoning for needing marriage confirmation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable moment. Michael's fake logic for his guilt-driven fishing expedition is perfectly cringey and quotable.

jokes.text: All the time I was thinking, 'I'm sleeping with your wife.' And you know who does that? James freakin' Bond.
jokes.setup: Michael describing shaking hands with the husband
jokes.punchline: Michael comparing his adultery to spy heroics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion. His transformation of shameful behavior into heroic fantasy is character-perfect and darkly funny.

jokes.text: And you know who does that? James freakin' Bond.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his deceptive handshake
jokes.punchline: Comparing his adultery to spy heroics
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Michael glamorizing his terrible behavior with pop culture

jokes.text: Why would you throw something wet at me? You love it.
jokes.setup: Kid throws orange slice at Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's confident assertion that the kid enjoyed it
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional confidence about his appeal to children

jokes.text: Well, then it wouldn't be the first time I stole something away from Coach Shane.
jokes.setup: Michael taking orange slices meant for the baseball team
jokes.punchline: Michael's cruel parallel between stealing food and stealing wives
jokes.explanation: Dark and economical parallel structure. Michael's pride in his theft/adultery comparison is perfectly crafted cruelty.

jokes.text: I'm awful, aren't I? Yes. That's pretty bad. I'm an awful guy! Ooh!
jokes.setup: Michael seeking validation that he's terrible
jokes.punchline: Michael's perverse pleasure in being confirmed as awful
jokes.explanation: Good character psychology showing Michael's twisted need for negative attention when positive is unavailable.

jokes.text: From now on, when I'm hungry, I am going to eat whatever I am hungry for.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his new philosophy
jokes.punchline: Treating basic eating as profound life change
jokes.explanation: Michael turning mundane decision into grand philosophical statement

jokes.text: That's Meredith's cake. It's her birthday. I don't care. I have an appetite for life!
jokes.setup: Michael taking someone else's birthday cake
jokes.punchline: Michael's grandiose justification for petty theft
jokes.explanation: Michael's philosophy applied to cake theft. The dramatic overstatement for minor selfishness is character-perfect.

jokes.text: You know what? I think you're attractive, and I wanna sleep with you. What about Kelly? You read my mind. Is this a joke? Yep.
jokes.setup: Michael proposing to Ryan as part of his new selfish philosophy
jokes.punchline: Michael's casual admission it was just a joke after the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Michael's boundary-pushing behavior. The anticlimatic revelation it's a joke is properly deflating.

jokes.text: You know what? I think you're attractive, and I wanna sleep with you. What about Kelly? You read my mind. Is this a joke? Yep.
jokes.setup: Ryan seemingly propositioning Kelly
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was just a cruel joke
jokes.explanation: Dark Ryan humor showing his cruelty to Kelly with efficient misdirection

jokes.text: Schrute sperm are strong, but they're no match for a grown Schrute man.
jokes.setup: Dwight planning to sabotage his own fertility for the contract
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarre logic about defeating his own reproductive capability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic treating reproduction as internal warfare. The absurd confidence is perfectly crafted character comedy.

jokes.text: He don't give an 'f' about nothin'!
jokes.setup: Michael refusing to refill the printer paper
jokes.punchline: Darryl's street commentary on Michael's new attitude
jokes.explanation: Good Darryl character moment. His appreciation for Michael's rebellion is well-delivered with good economy.

jokes.text: If I drink it, Cece drinks it six hours later.
jokes.setup: Discussion of energy drinks for tired Pam
jokes.punchline: Pam's specific knowledge of breastfeeding timing
jokes.explanation: Realistic parenting humor. The specific timing detail shows Pam's new mother expertise and exhaustion.

jokes.text: Sometimes I think about it when I'm trying to fall asleep at home.
jokes.setup: Description of secret warehouse sleeping area
jokes.punchline: The irony of thinking about work sleep spot while trying to sleep at home
jokes.explanation: Poignant working-class humor. The reversal of preferring work to home sleeping is both sad and funny.

jokes.text: A motel is dirty and it is sexy. Like me. And like Donna.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he's meeting Donna at a motel
jokes.punchline: Michael's grandiose self-description as dirty and sexy
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael delusion. His self-image as a sexy bad boy is perfectly delusional and cringey.

jokes.text: Because a motel is dirty and it is sexy. Like me. And like Donna.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why meeting at motel instead of condo
jokes.punchline: Describing himself and Donna as dirty and sexy
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional self-image with crude confidence

jokes.text: I could have stopped him.
jokes.setup: Michael leaving dramatically to meet his married girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Jim's understated claim he could have intervened but didn't
jokes.explanation: Good Jim deadpan moment. The casual admission of choosing not to help is economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I could have stopped him.
jokes.setup: Michael leaving dramatically to meet Donna
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact admission he chose not to intervene
jokes.explanation: Good Jim moment - acknowledging he's given up trying to control Michael

jokes.text: Stop kissing me. It's not in the contract.
jokes.setup: Angela and Dwight's contractual sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: Angela's businesslike rejection of romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela character moment. Reducing intimacy to contract terms is brilliantly cold and economical.

jokes.text: Are you warmed up? No. God, Angela, why is that always my responsibility?
jokes.setup: Angela and Dwight about to fulfill contract
jokes.punchline: Dwight complaining about foreplay responsibility
jokes.explanation: Awkward intimate moment treated like household chore division

jokes.text: How do I feel about breaking up with Donna? Good. Moral. I feel proud. Like a grownup.
jokes.setup: Michael in talking head after ending affair
jokes.punchline: Excessive self-praise for basic moral behavior
jokes.explanation: Michael's need for validation even for doing the right thing

jokes.text: And it was either living with myself or...being happy. And I picked... the... former.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his decision to break up with Donna
jokes.punchline: Michael's regretful admission he chose morality over happiness
jokes.explanation: Poignant Michael moment showing genuine moral struggle. The hesitation and word choice reveal real internal conflict.

tmdb_overview: Michael suspects Donna is cheating on him and puts P.I. Dwight Schrute on the case. Meanwhile, Andy is frustrated when no one takes his customer's complaint seriously.
